decisions.summary: Ship all 8 PRD §4.8 deliverables in one initiative — registry, primitive, Board card, Issue Detail drawer, Command Deck reconciliation, Pipeline rows, Agent cards, the 3 zero-UI actions, and the CLI↔dashboard parity test. Confirmed by user; matches CLAUDE.md 'Deliver Complete Features' and the issue body's 'all 8 items ship as one initiative'.

REPLACE the existing `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/commandDeckActions.ts` (25 ActionKey vocabulary) with a new canonical `issueActions` registry that matches PRD §4.8.1's catalog (~28 actions including plan/tell/pause/unpause/untroubled/sync-main/inspect/wipe/destroy/open). Delete `commandDeckActions.ts`, delete `commandDeckSurfaceRegistry.ts`, and rewrite ZoneActionStrip's phase-primary partitioning on top of the new registry. This is the 'single source of truth' the PRD asks for; coexistence would leave two parallel vocabularies. Confirmed by user.

Add a new server route `POST /api/issues/:id/beads/:beadId/inspect` (with optional `{ deep: true }` body) that spawns `spawnInspectAgent()` to verify a completed bead. PRD §4.8.1 flags this as 'verify/new endpoint'; the CLI command `pan inspect <id> --bead <beadId>` already exists but has no HTTP entry point. Confirmed by user.

Package the shared rendering as ONE component, `<IssueActionMenu issueId mode pinRight?>`, with three modes: `inline` (cluster only), `overflow-only` (⋯ button only), `hybrid` (1-2 inline + ⋯). Drawer additionally passes `pinRight={['view-pr','merge']}` to keep View PR and Merge on the right edge. Behind the component is one hook, `useIssueActions(issueId)`, that computes the partitioned layout from the registry + live state. Auto-decision: the user explicitly waived input on this React packaging detail.

Phase-primary inline action selection lives in the registry as a `phasePrimary: PipelinePhase[]` field per action; the hook filters and orders them per the table in PRD §4.8.3. This keeps the inline-vs-overflow decision data-driven, not hardcoded in the renderer — matching how the registry already encodes `enabledWhen`.

Destructive actions (`wipe`, `destroy`, `closeOut`, `reset-issue`) MUST require a confirm dialog — `useConfirm()` from `DialogProvider` for safe-ish destructive, the existing typed-confirm pattern in `ResetIssueButton`/`deep-wipe` for the irreversible ones. `IssueActionMenu` does not render destructive actions without a confirm interstitial. Merge stays human-only and is NOT in the registry as an automatable action — it's surfaced separately via the existing `<MergeButton>` and the drawer's pin-right slot.

Right-click on Board card opens the same menu as ⋯ (per PRD §4.8.3 'Right-click also opens the menu'). Right-click on the Issue Detail drawer body is OUT of scope — the drawer already exposes the full set in its footer; adding a context menu would be redundant and risks shadowing browser-native right-click on text.

Registry lives at `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/issueActions.ts`. Action handlers split into per-action client modules under `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/issueActions/` (one file per action group) so the registry stays narrow and the handlers stay testable. Existing dialog components (`PlanDialog`, `BeadsDialog`, `SwitchModelModal`, `PanOpenInPicker`) are imported but NOT rewritten — the menu just opens them.

`PanOpenInPicker` is currently defined but mounted nowhere. The `open` registry action invokes it as a modal/popover anchored to the menu. No changes to PanOpenInPicker itself in scope; mount-and-wire only.

CLI↔dashboard parity test lives at `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/__tests__/issueActions.parity.test.ts`. It reads the canonical list of issue-scoped `pan` verbs from a hardcoded array (the same list this plan enumerates — plan, start, swarm, tell, done, review/restart/reset, kill, pause, unpause, untroubled, recover, resume, sync-main, inspect, reopen, close, wipe, destroy, open) and asserts (a) each verb has a registry entry, (b) each registry entry renders on the drawer in some enabled-state fixture. Wired into `npm test`. Closes #243.

The IssueDrawer footer (`DrawerActionBar.tsx`) is the canonical full-coverage surface. Its inline phase-primary set adapts per phase (per PRD §4.8.3 phase-primary table). View PR and Merge are pinned right via the new `pinRight` prop. Reset/Stop are reachable via the menu like every other action — no special hardcoded Reset/Stop buttons.

hazards.summary: Replacing `commandDeckActions.ts` is a wide refactor — `ZoneActionStrip.tsx` (657 lines) consumes its ActionKey type via a large switch statement that translates keys to ~25 distinct button components. Each translation must be re-pointed at the new registry's action keys or absorbed into the menu's generic renderer.
hazards.mitigation: Do the registry+menu first as foundation beads (with inspection gate), THEN re-point ZoneActionStrip. ZoneActionStrip rewrite gets its own bead so reviewers can verify the action-coverage diff in isolation. Snapshot test the rendered action sets before/after on a fixed set of issue fixtures.

hazards.summary: Per-bead inspect server endpoint is new. The CLI's `spawnInspectAgent()` requires a workspace path, project key, issue ID, and bead ID. From the HTTP route we must derive workspace and project from the issue ID via `resolveProjectFromIssue()` and the standard workspace location. Mismatched project resolution would spawn an inspect into the wrong workspace.
hazards.mitigation: Mirror the path-resolution logic from `src/cli/commands/inspect.ts` lines 41-64 verbatim. Add an integration test that POSTs to `/api/issues/PAN-XXX/beads/<bead>/inspect` against a fixture workspace and asserts the right tmux session name was spawned.

hazards.summary: `DrawerActionBar.tsx` and `IssueDrawer.tsx` both contain `void COMMAND_DECK_SURFACE_REGISTRY;` — dead-code references to the file we're deleting. Several other components reference it too (KanbanBoard, BadgeBar, ContainerNode, ZoneActionStrip). Missing one will break the build.
hazards.mitigation: `grep -rn COMMAND_DECK_SURFACE_REGISTRY` before deletion; remove every reference in the same bead as the file deletion. Build runs in CI.

hazards.summary: Phase-primary inline set per PRD §4.8.3 (Plan…/Start agent…, Watch planning/Done planning, Tell agent…/Done, Open/Tell, Tell/Recover, Open/Request review, Recover/Tell, View PR/Merge, Close out…) does not perfectly match today's `getZoneAActions()` partitioning. Users may notice that the Command Deck's primary buttons change after reconciliation.
hazards.mitigation: Treat the PRD table as authoritative. Document the change in the IssueWorkbench test snapshots and in the §4.8 release notes section of the PR description. Acceptable behavior change per the issue's amendment note.

hazards.summary: `PanOpenInPicker` is unmounted today; we don't know whether its props/behavior actually work end-to-end. It might depend on dashboard config we haven't wired in (editor list, default editor preference).
hazards.mitigation: First bead that mounts it adds a manual-test sub-item: open the menu, click Open, verify a real editor launches. If broken, file a follow-up issue for fixing PanOpenInPicker itself — wiring is in scope; bug-fixing the picker is not.

hazards.summary: Right-click on Board cards may interfere with browser-native context-menu users rely on (Inspect Element, etc., especially in dev). Right-click should not absolutely block the browser default in production builds — only swap when the menu is actually being shown.
hazards.mitigation: Use `event.preventDefault()` inside the handler ONLY after computing the menu has at least one enabled action. Empty registry → fall through to browser default.

hazards.summary: `getZoneAActions()` and `flattenActions()` are imported by `commandDeckActions.test.ts`, `ZoneActionStrip.test.tsx`, `IssueWorkbench.test.tsx`, and other tests. Deleting these without updating tests will cascade test failures.
hazards.mitigation: Sweep tests in the same bead that deletes the old vocabulary. Some tests can map onto the new hook (`useIssueActions`); others (snapshots of inline button sets) should be rewritten against the new partitioning to reflect §4.8.3.

hazards.summary: `<IssueActionMenu>` opens existing dialogs (`PlanDialog`, `SwitchModelModal`, etc.) as side effects. The dialogs today are mounted at various levels (some in `App.tsx`, some inline in `KanbanBoard.tsx`). Opening them from a deeply-nested menu requires a global open/close mechanism.
hazards.mitigation: Use the existing `useDashboardStore` for dialog state where one is already wired, or a new dedicated `useIssueDialogStore` hook for the small set that lives inline. Document which dialogs use which mechanism in the registry handler files. Confirm with a render-and-click smoke test for each dialog in the foundation bead's acceptance criteria.

hazards.summary: The phase-primary mapping is keyed off pipeline phase, but today's `derivePipelineState()` in `commandDeckActions.ts` and the kanban's `IssueCard` phase derivation may produce different phase strings. The new registry must use one canonical phase classifier.
hazards.mitigation: Reuse the existing `derivePipelineState()` logic (move it into the new `issueActions.ts` or its own module). Have ONE phase classifier that BOTH the registry's `phasePrimary` filter and the Board card's verb badge consume — no duplicate phase logic.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1331: Restore the per-issue action surface — wire every `pan` verb into Board cards and the Issue Detail drawer (PAN-1148 §4.8)
Discovery complete. Decisions recorded; writing vBRIEF.
